news-global-econ.title: Bank of Baroda, India’s second-biggest state bank, aims to double size in 5 years
news-global-econ.description: Bank of Baroda plans to double its size in five years, focusing on global competitiveness and expanding its balance sheet.

news-global-econ.title: Honda's Costly EV Fiasco Drives First-Ever Annual Loss
news-global-econ.description: Honda announced a 423.9 billion yen ($2.7 billion) loss Thursday in a first-ever negative result for the fabled Japanese automaker, reportedly driven by heavy costs for its electric-vehicle plans underscored by President Donald Trump’s pro-U.S. manufacturing …

news-global-econ.title: Trump-Xi’s China summit is a defining test for America in the new Cold War
news-global-econ.description: Trump arrives in Beijing on May 14 facing discussions on semiconductors, Taiwan, Iran and AI as the U.S.-China rivalry reshapes the international order.

news-global-econ.title: MORNING GLORY: President Trump needs to finish the war against Iran once and for all
news-global-econ.description: Iran has reportedly regained operational access to most of its missile sites and launchers, according to leaked U.S. intelligence assessments.

news-global-econ.title: JONATHAN TURLEY: AOC's war on billionaires twists America's birth into a socialist myth
news-global-econ.description: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez claims the American Revolution was against billionaires, but the Founders believed in capitalism and free markets, Turley says.

news-global-soc.title: What Xi wants from Trump as Beijing seeks leverage in high-stakes summit
news-global-soc.description: President Donald Trump arrived in Beijing for a high-stakes summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping at a moment when both Washington and Beijing are trying to stabilize one of the world’s most consequential rivalries.

news-global-soc.title: Shakira, Madonna and BTS co-headline first-ever World Cup Final halftime show
news-global-soc.description: BTS, Madonna and Shakira will perform at the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final halftime show, curated by Coldplay's Chris Martin, the first ever for the tournament.
